channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Gonzaga beats No. 19 Saint Mary's 58-51 to capture West Coast Conference Tournament title
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The metrics — and oddsmakers — indicated Gonzaga was far better than its record, and there was little doubt the Bulldogs had the ability to once again compete at a high level. Maybe it was simply a matter of timing.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: No Donovan Mitchell? No problem for Cavaliers as they tie their team record with 15th straight win
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — The wins and accomplishments keep piling up for the NBA-leading Cleveland Cavaliers. They’ve been so frequent that players are having a hard time keeping track. “What did we do? Oh, we clinched the Central Division,” center Jarrett Allen said, chuckling. “That’s something.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN DULIK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tyrese Haliburton's stunning 4-point play gives the Pacers a 115-114 win over the Bucks
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Tyrese Haliburton made a fadeaway 3-pointer while being fouled and completed the four-point play with 3.4 seconds left, and the Indiana Pacers stunned Giannis Antetokounmpo and the Milwaukee Bucks 115-114 on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: MARK AMBROGI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Injured Brad Marchand returns to the Boston ice with the Panthers
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Brad Marchand stepped back onto the ice at the TD Garden on Tuesday — this time in a Panthers practice jersey as the longtime Bruin returned to Boston for the first time since he was traded to Florida.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Junior Bridgeman, businessman and basketball standout for Louisville and Milwaukee Bucks, dies at 71
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Junior Bridgeman, a basketball standout who led Louisville to a Final Four, starred for the NBA's Milwaukee Bucks and then launched an even more successful career as a businessman with stakes in restaurants, publishing and the Bucks franchise, died Tuesday. He was 71.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: NBA-leading Cavaliers beat Nets 109-104 for their second 15-game win streak of season
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — All-Star Darius Garland scored 18 of his 30 points in the fourth quarter and the NBA-leading Cleveland Cavaliers rallied for a 109-104 victory over the Brooklyn Nets on Tuesday night, matching the longest winning streak in franchise history at 15 games.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN DULIK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Former NBA star Dwight Howard blames LeBron James for his Lakers exit after championship run
item.description: Former NBA star Dwight Howard did not resign with the Lakers after he helped the franchise win a title, and he claims one of his ex-teammates is responsible.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dave Portnoy reveals 'weird' government call offering job; says he would've had to stop running Barstool
item.description: Barstool Sports founder Dave Portnoy revealed a government call he got offering a job, where he would've had to stop running his media brand. He described the call as "weird.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: After the milestone was first wiped out by an offsides review, MacKinnon finally earns 1,000th point
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Nathan MacKinnon appeared to have reached the 1,000-point milestone in the first period of Colorado's game against Chicago. His teammates even streamed onto the ice to celebrate his history-making assist. That turned out to be just a dress rehearsal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Josh Giddey scores season-high 29 points before injuring ankle and Bulls beat Pacers 121-103
item.description: Josh Giddey had a season-high 29 points along with 10 rebounds before leaving midway through the fourth quarter with a right ankle injury, and the Chicago Bulls won their third straight game, 121-103 over the Indiana Pacers on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: QB Sam Darnold agrees on a 3-year, $100.5 million deal with Seahawks, AP source says
item.description: The Seattle Seahawks found the replacement for Geno Smith, agreeing to a three-year, $100.5 million contract with Sam Darnold, a person with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press on Monday.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI and JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Luka, Lakers frustrated with officiating and themselves in a rough first game without LeBron James
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — LeBron James sat on the end of the Lakers' bench, wearing a red baseball cap while watching his team in the first game since his groin injury. The red on the court Monday night was in Luka Doncic's face, frustrated by his rocky play and angry with the officiating.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Paige Bueckers and UConn are healthy and rolling as her final March Madness approaches
item.description: UNCASVILLE, Conn. (AP) — Paige Bueckers is in a class by herself in the Big East, becoming the first player to win the most outstanding player of the conference tournament three times.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jets and QB Justin Fields agree to a 2-year, $40 million deal, AP source says
item.description: Justin Fields was passed over by the New York Jets four years ago in the NFL draft. Now he's expected to be their No. 1 quarterback. Fields and the Jets agreed Monday to a two-year contract worth $40 million, a person with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bills reward Allen with new contract after NFL MVP season. Deal is worth $330M, AP source says
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — NFL MVP Josh Allen was rewarded Sunday with a contract extension worth $330 million, including an NFL record $250 million of it guaranteed, which makes him among the league’s highest-paid players.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Christopher Bell holds off Denny Hamlin at Phoenix to win third straight NASCAR Cup Series race
item.description: Bell survived a late drag race with Hamlin to become the first NASCAR Cup Series driver to win three straight races in the NextGen car, edging his teammate by 0.049 seconds to win the second-closest race in Phoenix Raceway history on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Boys Soccer CIF Division IV SoCal State Regional Championship: Pacific Ridge 3, Central Union 2
item.description: The CIF Division IV Boys Soccer SoCal Regional State Championship featured two San Diego Section teams, as the Pacific Ridge Firebirds hosted the Central Union Spartans.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Soldier Field to host Premier League doubleheader this summer
item.description: The Premier League Summer Series is coming to Chicago this summer, featuring four Premier League clubs - AFC Bournemouth, Everton, Manchester United, and West Ham United - in a doubleheader at Soldier Field on Wednesday, July 30.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Myles Garrett becomes highest-paid non-QB in NFL history
item.description: Garrett, the 2023 AP DPOY, had asked for a trade last month but the Browns were adamant about keeping the 29-year-old star in Cleveland.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Oldest former MLB player, who once replaced Mickey Mantle on Yankees' roster, dies
item.description: Art Schallock got his shot in the major leagues after a struggling Mickey Mantle was optioned by New York in 1951.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Warriors star Stephen Curry reaches 25,000 career points
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Stephen Curry became the 26th player in NBA history to score 25,000 career points, reaching the milestone during the third quarter of the Golden State Warriors' 115-110 victory over the Detroit Pistons on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Stamkos finishes 14th career hat trick in OT to give Predators 3-2 win over Blackhawks
item.description: Steven Stamkos scored at 2:39 of overtime to complete his 14th career hat trick and the Nashville Predators beat the Chicago Blackhawks 3-2 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Real Salt Lake aims to keep winning as San Diego FC visits for the first time
item.description: Real Salt Lake returns to action at America First Field on Saturday night, facing MLS expansion side San Diego FC for the first time. Coming off a commanding 2-0 win over Seattle last weekend, RSL looks to build momentum and keep their shutout streak alive in front of their raucous home crowd.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Casey, ABC 4

item.title: Nikola Jokic makes NBA history with 30-20-20 triple-double in Nuggets' OT win over Suns
item.description: The three-time NBA MVP had the league's first 30-20-20 triple-double in the Denver Nuggets' 149-141 overtime victory over the Phoenix Suns on Friday night. Jokic had 31 points, 21 rebounds and a career-high 22 assists.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KELLY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Girls Basketball: Mater Dei Catholic 59, Carlsbad 52
item.description: Mater Dei Catholic winning and advancing to the Division III State Regional semifinals, 59-52 over Carlsbad,
item.dc:creator: Bri Savant, Fox 5

item.title: A's announce 3-year Las Vegas sponsorship as club looks toward future move
item.description: The Athletics will play in Northern California for at least the next three seasons, but will also represent their future home in Las Vegas and brought four of their star players to drive home that point.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Kenwood girls and boys basketball squads looking to make school history together
item.description: The Kenwood Girls Basketball team defeated Alton 61-55 Friday afternoon at the IHSA Class 4A semifinal to advance to the school’s first state championship game.
item.dc:creator: Josh Frydman, WGN 9

item.title: San Francisco State University cutting men's soccer and 2 other sports
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- San Francisco State University announced plans on Thursday to cut three sports programs as part of its budget planning. The announced cuts follow a budget meeting last week that was "particularly difficult," according to an open letter posted by SFSU President Lynn Mahoney.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: White Sox reassign top 2 prospects to minor-league camp, team announces Thursday
item.description: GLENDALE, Ariz. — The White Sox reassigned two of their top prospects to minor-league camp, one a west suburban native, the team announced Thursday morning prior to its spring training game against the San Francisco Giants.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Boys Volleyball: The Bishop's School 3, La Jolla 1
item.description: Boys Volleyball matchup is the battle of Pearl street between The Bishop's school Knights and La Jolla Vikings.
item.dc:creator: Allison Edmonds, Fox 5

item.title: Chris Collins shares special basketball bond with son Ryan at Northwestern
item.description: EVANSTON, Ill. - Two hours before tipoff Monday at Welsh-Ryan Arena, Ryan Collins had the ball in his hands, showing off his shot, a similar scene to another coach’s son some four decades earlier. “He shows off before the game, just like I used to do,” said Northwestern head coach Chris Collins.
item.dc:creator: Josh Frydman, WGN 9

item.title: Reports: Bears trade for all-pro Chiefs OL Joe Thuney
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. — The Bears have reportedly traded for four-time Super Bowl champion guard Joe Thuney from the Chiefs. According to multiple reports, the Bears are sending back a 2026 fourth-round pick for the 32-year-old guard.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9
